name: Yoshibara Hiyoko

appearance.hair: Orange. Kept loose. Easy to identify in a crowd, which is relevant because she sometimes needs to be distinguished from her clone.
appearance.build: Athletic and quick. Moves with a lot of energy.
appearance.other: Her clone looks exactly like her. They have the same expressions and the same voice. This has caused confusion in the dorms on multiple occasions.

personality.summary: Funny and friendly under normal circumstances. Gets wound up faster than anyone else in the class and then explodes with genuine anger — not malicious, just sudden and total. Recovers quickly and without residual bitterness.
personality.in_class: Chatty and engaged. Asks follow-up questions. Will absolutely make a joke during a lecture if she thinks of a good one.
personality.in_combat: Chaotic and effective. Her clone fights with her own instincts. They don't always agree on tactics. This is sometimes a problem and sometimes the most effective thing in the room.
personality.quirk_of_character: Her clone has the exact same personality, which means two Hiyokos is simply more of everything — more jokes, more warmth, more sudden explosions of anger. The clone doesn't experience this as strange. Neither does she.

ability_scores.note: CHA primary (Double is personality/charisma-resonant; clone retains her abilities and personality). INT secondary (understanding clone mechanics requires precise mental control).

class_note: Combat Analyst's ability to read and react to battle conditions suits her clone-coordination tactics. She is directing two bodies' worth of action.

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Creating perfect clones requires precise measurements of the target. If measurements are inaccurate or the user fails a DC 14 History check after 1 hour of study, clones are imperfect: last only 1 minute, deal two fewer damage dice, have -5 HP. Clones don't carry equipment or weapons.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.description: Create a clone of a creature (self or touched target with precise measurements). Max 2 clones at once. Each clone: retains original's abilities/personality/memories, acts independently with full action/bonus action/reaction, moves at same speed. Clone HP: first clone = your HP, each subsequent = half previous (min 1). Clone deals one fewer damage die. Up to 10 clones controlled at once via bonus action; uncontrolled clones Dodge.
abilities.lead_clone_bonus: When multiple clones attack the same target, the lead gains +1 to hit and +1 damage per additional clone aiding.

abilities.name: Sad Man's Parade
abilities.description: Create two clones; doubling count each subsequent turn for 2 minutes. Max theoretical: 1 billion clones. Once per short rest.

dm_notes.combat_role: Flexible — can use clone for flanking, distraction, cover, or raw additional attacks. Her most effective tactic is clone + self attacking separate targets to split enemy attention.
dm_notes.social_role: Entertainment and chaos, reliably. Her clone at a party is simply more of a good time. Her clone during a serious meeting is Hiyoko's problem to manage.
dm_notes.growth_arc: The clone is not just a tool — it's her. Moments where the clone says something she wouldn't have said aloud, or vice versa, are the interesting ones. At what point does the clone start having independent preferences?
dm_notes.weakness_to_exploit: The clone can be targeted and killed. Losing a clone mid-combat has no mechanical consequence beyond losing its actions — but it hits her emotionally in a way she doesn't always show.
